ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for hot chocolate beverage; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard cup serving of Signature Hot Chocolate is approximately 240 ml (248 g), providing about 104 kcal, 25.7 g carbohydrate, and 24.7 g sugar.

nutrient_source.title: Signature Hot Chocolate — HPB Singapore Foodo Database (G00010295)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo canonical record G00010295 for Signature Hot Chocolate. All per-100g values are sourced from this record with confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; Diabetes Care 2008
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific branded Signature Hot Chocolate. GI estimated at 60 (medium) based on the closest analogous food: sweetened chocolate milk / hot cocoa beverage with added sugar, as documented in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care). The beverage is predominantly sugar-sweetened (9.94g sugar/100g) with negligible fibre, supporting a medium-to-high GI estimate. GL per serving calculated as: GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving / 100 = 60 × 25.7 / 100 = 15.4 (serving size 248g).

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (9.94g/100g) in a sugar-sweetened beverage independently elevates blood pressure risk; sodium is low at 3mg/100g but does not offset the sugar-driven cardiovascular risk.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this sugar-sweetened hot chocolate, as regular consumption of beverages with >5g sugar/100g is independently associated with elevated blood pressure per AHA 2018 guidelines; request an unsweetened or reduced-sugar cocoa alternative.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 9.94g sugar/100g and zero dietary fibre; liquid sugar is absorbed rapidly, causing acute postprandial blood glucose elevation.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with type 2 diabetes should avoid this beverage as it qualifies as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) with 9.94g sugar/100g and zero fibre, causing rapid glycaemic excursions; substitute with unsweetened cocoa powder in low-fat milk to reduce sugar load while retaining cocoa flavour.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High free-sugar content (9.94g/100g) with negligible fibre and potassium; regular SSB consumption is associated with increased triglycerides, visceral adiposity, and cardiovascular event risk.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit this beverage due to its high free-sugar content (9.94g/100g), which contributes to hypertriglyceridaemia and weight gain when consumed regularly; choose unsweetened cocoa preparations as cocoa flavanols may confer modest cardioprotective benefit without the added sugar burden.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High sugar content (9.94g/100g) promotes pro-inflammatory cytokine activity; no anti-inflammatory nutrients present (zero fibre, negligible potassium, no omega-3 or polyphenols in this sweetened preparation).
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this sweetened hot chocolate as the high added sugar content promotes systemic inflammation; request unsweetened cocoa with plain milk to access cocoa polyphenols without the pro-inflammatory sugar load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Signature Hot Chocolate is a sweetened hot beverage made with steamed milk, vanilla syrup, and mocha-flavoured chocolate syrup, served without whipped cream. It is a Western-style café drink popular in Singapore and internationally. The beverage is predominantly carbohydrate-driven, with 10.36g carbohydrate and 9.94g sugar per 100g, reflecting the high proportion of flavoured syrups used in its preparation. Protein content is effectively zero (confirmed per HPB source G00010295), and fat is minimal at 0.25g/100g, indicating the drink is low in fat but high in free sugars. Energy density is moderate at 42 kcal/100g, yielding approximately 104 kcal per standard 240ml cup. Dietary fibre is absent, and sodium is very low at 3mg/100g. The beverage qualifies as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) under Singapore HPB and WHO definitions. Clinically, it is unsuitable for individuals with type 2 diabetes due to rapid glycaemic impact, and should be consumed with caution by those managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease due to its high free-sugar content. Unsweetened cocoa powder in low-fat milk is a lower-sugar alternative that preserves cocoa flavour and polyphenol content. The glycemic index is estimated at 60 (medium) based on analogous sweetened chocolate milk data from the University of Sydney GI Database.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a hot chocolate?
Is hot chocolate high in sugar?
What is the GI of hot chocolate?
Is hot chocolate suitable for hypertension?
Is hot chocolate a sugar-sweetened beverage?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo canonical record G00010295 with confidence 0.95. The record is marked as reviewed with provenance canonical. GI is an analogous estimate (medium confidence for GI component only) based on University of Sydney GI Database data for sweetened chocolate milk beverages.
notes: protein_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo record G00010295; this is a syrup-and-milk beverage where the protein contribution from the small amount of milk is below the reporting threshold in the HPB dataset — confirmed zero per approved source. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero as this is a liquid beverage with no whole-food fibre sources. cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero per HPB source. magnesium_mg is confirmed zero per HPB source. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, vitamin panel, and moisture data not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database for this beverage record as of 2026. food_subgroup in input was listed as 'Coffee' which appears to be a data entry error; corrected to 'Hot Chocolate / Cocoa Beverages' as this is a chocolate-based beverage with no coffee component per the product description. This is a Western-style café beverage served in Singapore; multilingual local-name fields (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) are not applicable as this is not a traditional Singaporean dish with established local-language names — the English name is universally used across all languages in the Singapore café context. saturated_fat_g set to 0.0 as the HPB source reports total fat of 0.25g/100g with no saturated fat breakdown; given the minimal fat content, saturated fat is effectively zero or trace.
